meta_title: For Students: Claude Code skills setup to Solve no project ideas (Intermediate Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to Claude Code skills setup and overcome no project ideas. A Intermediate-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: Claude Code Skills to Solve No Project Ideas
content.seo_description: Overcome the 'no project ideas' block with this intermediate guide. Master Claude Code and Antigravity to build, iterate, and launch your first AI app.
content.answer_block: The 'no project ideas' paralysis stems from a lack of iterative momentum. By leveraging Claude Code to automate boilerplate and Antigravity to architect logic, you transform abstract concepts into functional prototypes. This workflow shifts your focus from 'what to build' to 'how to engineer impact' through rapid, AI-assisted development cycles.
content.intro:
The blank editor is the most intimidating antagonist a student dev faces. You have the theoretical knowledge, the curiosity, and the drive, yet the paralysis of choice keeps your portfolio empty. That 'no project ideas' wall isn't a lack of creativity; it’s a lack of a high-velocity execution framework.

Today, we shatter that wall. We are moving beyond simple prompts into a professional-grade engineering workflow. By integrating Claude Code with Antigravity, you gain an unfair advantage: the ability to architect, debug, and ship production-ready features in hours, not weeks. This is how you stop dreaming and start building.

steps.title: Define the High-Value Problem
steps.description: Don't build a generic to-do list. Use Antigravity to find a niche pain point in a specific industry, ensuring your project demonstrates real-world utility.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze current trends in developer tools and identify three high-frequency pain points for freelance web developers that could be solved with a specialized AI agent.
steps.claude_code_command: claude terminal --prompt 'Create a README.md outlining a project architecture for a tool that solves [identified pain point].'

steps.title: Generate the Scaffold
steps.description: Stop wrestling with configuration. Use Claude Code to initialize a robust project structure that follows modern best practices.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Design a clean, modular folder structure for a Node.js project using TypeScript and Zod for schema validation.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Initialize a project scaffold with Vite, TypeScript, and Tailwind CSS, following the modular architecture defined in my specs.'

steps.title: Implement Core Logic
steps.description: Focus on the business logic while the AI handles the repetitive boilerplate and type definitions.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Draft a robust API service layer that handles authentication and rate-limiting for an AI-integrated application.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Implement the API service layer. Ensure all functions are type-safe and include comprehensive error handling.'

steps.title: Automated Testing Suites
steps.description: Never ship broken code. Use Claude Code to generate unit tests that validate your primary project features.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a comprehensive test plan for the core business logic of my AI app, focusing on edge cases and potential API failures.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Generate Vitest unit tests for the core logic implemented in the service layer. Aim for 80% coverage.'

steps.title: Refine and Deploy
steps.description: Polish your code for production and prepare for deployment, ensuring your project is ready for your portfolio.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Audit the current codebase for performance bottlenecks and security vulnerabilities. Provide a list of recommended optimizations.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Perform a code review and refactor for performance. Then, generate a deployment script for Vercel.'

pro_tips: Treat Claude Code as a junior pair programmer, not a magic button; always review the generated diffs before committing.
Use Antigravity to generate 'negative test cases' to harden your app against malicious user inputs.
Keep your context windows lean by modularizing your files before invoking Claude Code commands.
Always document the 'why' behind architectural decisions in your commit messages for better portfolio storytelling.
Leverage Claude Code to write your project's technical documentation; it's the most overlooked skill for landing junior roles.
content.next_steps: Once your project is functional, deploy it to a live environment and add it to your GitHub portfolio. Create a 60-second Loom video demonstrating how your tool solves the initial problem, then share it on LinkedIn to build your professional brand.
content.conversion_block: You now have the technical blueprint to outpace your peers and build software that matters. Don't let this knowledge sit idle; turn your next idea into a reality. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
